core_statement: Visual perception is probabilistic and interpretive, not objective truth. Review quality is about whether human answers are well-grounded in visible evidence, expressed consistently, and reusable across captures.

good_review: supplemental screenshots used only as context
evidence refs are complete and specific
notes are short, concrete, and evidence-based
contradictions are explicit when visible evidence conflicts
answer can be compared consistently with other reviewers
low_signal_review: answer repeats metadata instead of visible evidence
notes are vague, generic, or subjective
high confidence despite weak or missing evidence
partial answers used where a concrete judgment is required
same generic phrase used across multiple questions
governance metadata treated as visual evidence
brand knowledge used instead of screenshot evidence

items: frequent use of uncertain without explanation
conflicting answers across related questions
notes that hedge without naming the visible issue
high confidence paired with missing evidence refs
contradiction notes without a concrete visible conflict
repeated unresolved outcomes without stating what is missing
treating supplemental screenshots as replacements
question combines multiple judgments at once
question mixes observation and interpretation
question can only be answered using hidden metadata
question asks for a category label when only a partial cue is visible
question does not define binary vs graded expectations
question is impossible to answer from a single viewport
high confidence with missing screenshots
high confidence on an inherently ambiguous question
confidence assigned from brand familiarity
confidence copied across questions without re-evaluating evidence
confidence treated as a brand quality score
confidence used to mask unresolved evidence

good: references the evidence that caused the contradiction
noisy: states something is wrong without showing why
uses contradiction when the case is unresolved
contradicts metadata rather than visible evidence
turns every disagreement into a contradiction

items: partial can mean some evidence visible or some uncertainty remaining
partial can hide a real contradiction
partial can be used when the question should be binary
partial can be mistaken for agreement when it means weak support
confidence thresholds change without explanation
similar cases receive different outcomes across sessions
metadata starts being treated as evidence
notes become shorter, vaguer, or more generic
unresolved or contradicted states are no longer used
raw viewport ignored and supplemental image overtrusted
evidence too weak or broken to support a meaningful answer
questions too broad and absorb multiple judgments into one field
reviewers use different mental models for the same question
confidence is inflated
contradiction and unresolved states are underused
notes are too vague to support analysis
metadata is interpreted as evidence
case-specific guidance changes the meaning of stable questions
viewport usable overlaps with materially obstructed
clean attempt exists overlaps with clean attempt is supplemental
claim supported overlaps with review outcome confirmed
category cues visible overlaps with product or service presence visible

completeness_metrics: contradiction or unresolved fields used appropriately

evidence_adequacy: enough context exists to answer the question without guessing
supplemental clean attempt or full-page capture available when needed
evidence_inadequacy: question cannot be answered from what is visible
screenshot_quality_adequacy: are legible enough to support the question being asked
poor_screenshot_quality: loses the distinction between raw and supplemental evidence

onboarding_requirements: can identify when evidence is insufficient
can distinguish raw viewport from supplemental images
can avoid using metadata as the primary evidence source

strong: The raw viewport clearly shows the modal, so the obstruction is visible and the claim is contradicted.
The clean attempt exists, but it does not materially change the raw viewport reading, so it remains supplemental.
The page shows visible category cues, but not enough to confirm the stronger claim, so this is unresolved.
weak: Confidence high because the page looks familiar.

ingestion_enablement_criteria: acceptable reviewer agreement on repeated cases
evidence adequacy matching the question set
evaluation_heuristics: penalize high confidence on weak evidence
favor explicit contradiction handling when visible evidence conflicts
penalize partial answers where binary is required
check whether the raw viewport was inspected first
check whether evidence refs match the answer
future_qa_workflow: compare against gold or adjudicated set
gate ingestion until quality thresholds are met
